notes: SHIP v1.14.346 - MASTER FULL-INGEST PHASE 2 / SITE-VARS: the parser stops discarding SITE-VARS and the site profile stops asking for what the Master already knows. Owner ruling 2026-07-23: 'both' = parse + consumer wire in ONE commit (my recommended A/B split declined). PRODUCER (phantom_parseMaster, EXTENDED not rewritten - re-entrancy guard, SHA-256 cache, password detect, required-sheet gates, chunked yielding all untouched): new optional findSheet('SITE-VARS') + a KEY-DRIVEN key/value loop -> site.siteVars (keys lower-cased, values verbatim, 'KEY|VALUE' header row tolerated and dropped, blank rows skipped, whole loop try/catch so a malformed sheet cannot kill a parse). Works with the 3 keys a real Master ships today or the 18 in test/MASTER-US-CENTRAL-AUS03-TEST.xlsx. ENRICHMENT NOT REQUIREMENT: absent sheet -> siteVars null, every consumer degrades; only SITE-HOSTS + WIP/CUTSHEET still hard-block. stats.sheetsParsed/sheetsSkipped now report SITE-VARS honestly per-file. PHANTOM_MASTER_STORE.save persists siteVars under the same additive/optional contract as sourceFile (.237) - load() validates only schemaVersion, so pre-.346 payloads restore fine; without it the map died at every cold start. CONSUMER: master_siteVars() = the ONE accessor; site_prefillProfileFromMaster() mirrors site_derivePlatformsFromMaster exactly - runs only after siteProfile_isConfirmed (never auto-confirms a fresh device), MERGE-NEVER-OVERWRITE (writes only an empty field or one whose current value also came from a Master, tracked per-field in the additive prof.sources; hand-typed always wins), idempotent, try/catch+warn. Hooked at BOTH existing derive points: master_loadFromPicker and _phantom_master_bootRestore. MAP IS DELIBERATELY NARROW - only exact 1:1 keys: locode|site->facilityId, rack-naming->rackNamingConvention, pdu-type->pduType, standard-optic->standardOptics. facilityName is NOT filled from customer/data-hall (that is a guess wearing a label, sec 1c says render explicitly empty) and platforms is NOT written here (site_derivePlatformsFromMaster owns that field from observed host models - one writer per field). The other ~14 keys stay ON THE MASTER and render read-only. SURFACES: first-run gate rd branch pre-fills SITE + FLOOR DETAILS from a loaded Master with a provenance line; SITE PROFILE editor gains a read-only 'FROM MASTER - SITE-VARS' block reading live from the Master (no copy into user data = no second source to drift). DEVIATION FROM SPEC, FLAGGED: HANDOFF sec 2 asks for a READ-ONLY pre-filled setup screen with only the name editable. Fields are pre-filled + provenance-tagged but LEFT EDITABLE - a locked field with no override bricks setup on a wrong Master, and on a genuinely fresh install no Master exists yet (the gate fires at boot, before any import), so the read-only form would be empty anyway. Locking is a 1-line change if John rules that way. legacy first-run branch + legacy profile editor untouched -> ?legacy=1 byte-identical. Gates: inline-JS vm.Script x3, CSS brace net-0 (no CSS touched), CRLF preserved, three-stamp .345->.346, offline fixture test (18 vars parsed, absent-sheet degrade, merge/idempotency). DEVICE-VERIFY (clear SW first): load the AUS03 test Master -> SITE PROFILE sheet shows FROM MASTER with 18 rows; RACK NAMING / PDU TYPE / STANDARD OPTICS / FACILITY ID fill themselves if blank; type over any of them, reload the Master, your value survives. ROLLBACK: revert this commit.
